leadText: The Rare Bone (Japanese: きちょうなホネ Valuable Bone) is a valuable item introduced in Generation IV.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] The Rare Bone serves no practical purpose other than to be sold. In Pokémon Diamond, Pearl , and Platinum , the Rare Bone counts as a Fossil towards Fossil count when dug up in the Underground .

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description D P Pt HG SS A bone that is extremely valuable for Pokémon archaeology. It can be sold for a high price to shops. B W B2 W2 A bone that is extremely valuable for Pokémon archeology. It can be sold for a high price to shops. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA A rare bone that is extremely valuable for the study of Pokémon archeology. It can be sold for a high price to shops. S V A rare bone that is extremely valuable for the study of Pokémon archeology. It can be sold at a high price to shops.

sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 Price 1.2 Effect 1.3 Description 1.4 Acquisition 1.4.1 Distribution 1.4.1.1 As a held item In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] Price [ edit | edit source ] Games Cost Sell price D P Pt HG SS W B2 W2 X Y OR AS BD SP N/A $ 5000 B $ 30000 $ 5000 S M US UM Sw Sh S V N/A $ 2,500 In the Generation V games, the Rare Bone can be sold to the Rare Bone maniac on Route 18 for $ 10000. In Pokémon Sword and Shield , the Rare Bone can be sold to the valuable item buyer in Stow-on-Side for $ 11,111. Effect [ edit | edit source ] The Rare Bone serves no practical purpose other than to be sold. In Pokémon Diamond, Pearl , and Platinum , the Rare Bone counts as a Fossil towards Fossil count when dug up in the Underground . sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl , when found in the Grand Underground , the Rare Bone uses a whitened alternative of the artwork provided by the Pokémon Global Link . It is the only item in the game to use a sprite that is not identical to its Global Link counterpart. Until Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, the Rare Bone and Thick Club shared the same Bag sprite.
